bullets.summary: Nvidia reported Q2 FY2026 revenue of $46.7B, up 56% year-over-year and up 6% sequentially, beating analyst consensus of $46.1B. Data-center segment drove the beat; Blackwell GPU shipments ramped materially in the quarter.

Nvidia guided Q3 FY2026 revenue to $54.0B (+/-2%), implying continued sequential acceleration. The company excluded any H20 shipments to China from the outlook following a U.S. government license requirement imposed in April 2025, representing a material export-control overhang.

Nvidia holds an estimated 80-92% market share in AI accelerators. Blackwell GPUs were described as sold out through the next 12 months on the Q2 FY2026 earnings call, signaling demand significantly exceeds supply.
bullets.source: Yahoo Finance (Q2 FY2026 earnings call transcript)

bullets.summary: Microsoft reported Q4 FY2025 (quarter ending June 30, 2025) revenue of $76.4B, up 18% year-over-year; Azure and other cloud services grew 39%, driven by AI workload demand from enterprise customers.
Microsoft committed to invest $80B in AI-enabled data centers globally in calendar 2025, with 80% of Fortune 500 companies using Azure AI Foundry for AI workloads as of Q4 FY2025.

Microsoft launched Fairwater, its largest AI datacenter to date, in September 2025, together with a new Atlanta facility, forming what the company called an 'AI superfactory' for planet-scale inference capacity.

Alphabet reported Q2 2025 revenue of $96.4B, up 14% year-over-year, beating the $93.98B Wall Street estimate. Google Cloud grew 32% to $13.6B, led by AI Infrastructure and Generative AI Solutions. EPS of $2.31 beat by 5%.

Alphabet raised its 2025 capital expenditure plan to approximately $85B, up from prior guidance, signaling continued acceleration in AI infrastructure investment. The number of Google Cloud deals exceeding $250M doubled year-over-year in Q2.

Rocket Lab reported Q2 2025 record revenue of $144M, up 36% year-over-year. Gross profit was $46.4M (vs $27.2M in Q2 2024). Five Electron launches were completed in the quarter, including two launches from the same site within two days.
bullets.source: TradingView News (Rocket Lab Q2 2025 Financial Results)

bullets.summary: On August 5, 2025, Rocket Lab successfully launched its 69th Electron mission, deploying a satellite for iQPS as part of a multi-launch contract. A second mission ('Live, Laugh, Launch') for a confidential commercial customer deployed five satellites to 655km orbit later in August.

On September 29, 2025, Rocket Lab announced a second multi-launch contract with Synspective for 10 dedicated Electron launches, bringing total upcoming Synspective missions to 21 — the largest single-customer Electron backlog to date.

Chinese startup Unitree launched its R1 humanoid robot in July 2025 at $5,900 — a price point previously considered years away — signaling sharp manufacturing cost compression in the humanoid segment. BOTZ holds robotics automation companies exposed to this trend.

China's robotics sector saw 610 investment deals totaling 50 billion yuan ($7B) in the first nine months of 2025, up 250% year-over-year. Q3 alone saw 243 deals, up 102% from the prior year, reflecting intensifying capital formation across humanoid and industrial robotics.

The global robotics market was valued at approximately $108B in 2025. BOTZ (Global X Robotics & AI ETF) drew over $19B in net ETF-category flows over the prior 12 months, per Morningstar, reflecting broad investor interest in the robotics/AI automation theme.
bullets.source: Global X ETFs / ETF.com (BOTZ fund page)

bullets.summary: The Defiance QTUM Quantum Computing ETF surpassed $2B in assets under management on September 2, 2025, holding a 5-star Morningstar rating (as of June 30, 2025). The fund holds 71 quantum computing stocks including D-Wave, IonQ, and Rigetti.

Quantum computing companies raised $3.77B in equity funding in the first nine months of 2025, nearly triple the $1.3B raised in all of 2024. Error-correction progress was reported by QuEra, Alice & Bob, Microsoft, Google, IBM, Quantinuum, IonQ, Rigetti, and others.

Uranium ETFs surged in June 2025 on announcements that Meta and Constellation Energy agreed to new AI data-center power deals. An Oklo-linked deal was also announced in June 2025, underscoring the AI-to-nuclear power narrative driving the sector.
bullets.source: AInvest (Atomic Upside: Why 2025 IS Nuclear's Investment Renaissance)

bullets.summary: As of October 1, 2025 (boundary), NUKZ held 44 nuclear energy and fuel companies with $729M in net assets. The ETF's 52-week price ranged from $42.10 to $75.03, indicating significant appreciation from lows driven by AI power-demand narrative.

U.S. executive orders targeting a quadrupling of nuclear capacity by 2050 and streamlining of NRC regulation were in effect in 2025. AWS secured a 17-year, 1.92 GW PPA with Talen Energy's Susquehanna nuclear plant, investing $20B in Pennsylvania AI infrastructure.
bullets.source: IAEA Bulletin / SMR Data Centers coverage

bullets.summary: The Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points on September 17, 2025, setting the federal funds rate target range to 4.00-4.25%. The vote was 11-1. The cut followed a prolonged pause driven by uncertainty over tariff-driven inflation; the Fed signaled the cut moved policy toward a more neutral stance.

AGG (iShares Core U.S. Aggregate Bond ETF) tracks investment-grade U.S. bonds including Treasuries, agencies, CMBS, ABS, and corporates. The September 2025 Fed rate cut was the first easing action since pausing in 2025, a positive marginal signal for intermediate-duration bond prices.

BIL (SPDR Bloomberg 1-3 Month T-Bill ETF) holds short-duration Treasuries and closely tracks the upper bound of the federal funds rate. Following the September 17, 2025 Fed 25bp cut to 4.00-4.25%, BIL's yield stepped down modestly; it still offered approximately 4.0-4.25% annualized yield with near-zero duration risk.

Morgan Stanley noted in September 2025 that a Fed rate cut was expected but 'not assured,' and that money market fund yields would remain relatively elevated for longer even in an easing cycle, supporting cash-equivalent instruments like BIL through year-end.

Gold set a Q3 2025 peak of $3,858.41 per ounce on September 30, 2025, rising over 15% during the quarter. Total gold demand in Q3 2025 reached 1,313 tonnes (highest on record in World Gold Council data), with demand value jumping 44% year-over-year to a record $146B.
bullets.source: World Gold Council (Gold Demand Trends Q3 2025)

bullets.summary: ETF gold buying of 222 tonnes and bar/coin demand of 316 tonnes drove Q3 demand. Central bank buying was 220 tonnes, up 28% quarter-over-quarter. Key demand drivers: geopolitical tensions, Trump tariff uncertainty, weakening USD, and declining real interest rates following the September Fed cut.
bullets.source: World Gold Council (Gold Demand Trends Q3 2025)

bullets.summary: VIG (Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF) tracks companies with records of dividend growth; it posted approximately 14% year-to-date return through Q3 2025, outperforming its peer average while maintaining approximately 12% lower volatility.

The September 17, 2025 Fed rate cut (25bp to 4.00-4.25%) was a mild tailwind for dividend-growth equities as the rate on competing short-duration cash instruments declined marginally, supporting the relative appeal of dividend-paying large caps in VIG.

AI.rationale: All three AI-wave tickers (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL) reported accelerating, real revenue from AI workloads in Q2-Q4 FY2025: NVDA revenue +56% YoY to $46.7B with Blackwell sold out, Azure +39%, Google Cloud +32%. Hyperscalers collectively committed over $165B in 2025 AI datacenter capex. Revenue growth is compounding and demand exceeds supply — adoption is translating into reported dollars, not just announcements.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: RKLB is the primary signal: Q2 2025 revenue of $144M (+36% YoY) shows real commercial progress, and the Synspective 10-launch contract (Sept 29, 2025) adds to a growing backlog. However, the company remains unprofitable, the sector is still small and under-owned, and volume is measured in individual launches rather than mass-market scale. Real progress is visible in industry data — revenue, launches, contracts — but stock prices reflect early rather than mainstream positioning.

robotics.rationale: The Unitree R1 at $5,900 (July 2025) and $7B in Chinese robotics investment in the first 9 months of 2025 (+250% YoY) demonstrate that real progress is accumulating in cost compression and capital formation. However, mass commercial deployment of humanoid robots has not yet occurred; Tesla's 5,000-unit target for 2025 is orders of magnitude below any mainstream labor-substitution threshold. The thesis is early and real, but not yet compounding in revenue or stock prices.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: The AI-data-center power-demand narrative is translating into real capital commitments: AWS signed a 17-year, 1.92 GW PPA with Talen Energy; Meta and Constellation announced deals in June 2025; U.S. executive orders targeted quadrupling nuclear capacity. NUKZ appreciated from a 52-week low of $42.10 to a high of $75.03. New AI-demand-linked PPAs are real revenue events for nuclear utilities, not just enthusiasm. Adoption of nuclear-power agreements is compounding, even if new reactor construction is years away.

quantum.rationale: QTUM crossed $2B AUM on September 2, 2025, and quantum companies raised $3.77B in the first nine months of 2025 — nearly triple the full-year 2024 total. Error-correction research is progressing across multiple vendors. However, commercial quantum advantage for real business problems has not been demonstrated as of Q3 2025; the sector is pre-revenue at scale. Investment is growing rapidly but stocks remain under-owned relative to AI. Classic buildup profile: real research milestones, rising capital, not yet in broad portfolios.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, and VIG are macro/asset-class instruments with no single wave thesis. Macro context as of 2025-09-30: Fed began easing with a 25bp cut (Sept 17), gold at a record $3,858 driven by safe-haven demand, and dividend equities performing steadily. No wave classification is appropriate.
